reasons: Franco can suppress Badang and interrupt the value of his wall-combo damage window.
Franco can punish Badang after he commits into melee range.

proof.summary: Badang wants to commit with wall control and repeated-hit burst, while Franco can suppress him and interrupt the damage window.
worksBestWhen: Franco saves Bloody Hunt for Badang's commit.
Franco's team bursts Badang while he is suppressed.
failureCases: Franco wastes suppression before Badang commits.

reasons: Kaja can drag Badang away before or after his wall-combo setup.
Suppression is reliable when Badang commits too deep and loses exit options.

proof.summary: Badang wants a stationary burst window near his obstacle, while Kaja can suppress and pull him away from the target area.
worksBestWhen: Allies collapse while Badang is displaced.
failureCases: Badang forces Kaja to use ultimate defensively on another target.
Kaja suppresses after Badang has already finished his burst.

reasons: Diggie helps allies survive Badang's control chain by reducing the value of the lock window.
Badang is weaker when the target escapes or receives protection before Fist Crack finishes.

proof.summary: Badang wants to keep enemies trapped near his obstacle, while Diggie can protect nearby allies with cleanse and control immunity during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Badang's real wall combo.
Diggie stays near the ally Badang wants to trap.
failureCases: Badang baits Diggie's ultimate before committing.

reasons: Valir can push Badang away before he reaches the wall-combo angle.
Badang becomes less threatening if he cannot keep the target pinned near an obstacle.

proof.summary: Badang wants to enter close range and trap targets, while Valir can knock him back and reset the spacing before the combo is completed.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Badang's approach.
Valir positions away from walls and tight corridors.
failureCases: Valir wastes knockback before Badang commits.

reasons: Wanwan can remove control effects from herself and reposition, making Badang's lock window harder to complete.
This is execution-dependent because she must react before the full wall burst finishes.

proof.summary: Badang wants to trap and burst a target in place, while Wanwan can cleanse control effects and use mobility to escape the setup.
worksBestWhen: Wanwan saves Needles in Flowers for Badang's real control window.
Wanwan keeps distance from walls and obstacles.
failureCases: Badang catches Wanwan before she can react.
Wanwan is trapped in terrain with no safe escape path.
